content.problemStatement:
Write a program to print all the **leaders** in the array. An element is a leader if it is strictly greater than all the elements to its right side. And the rightmost element is always a leader.


Input Format

The first line contains NNN, the number of elements in an array.
The second line contains NNN integers, denoting the elements in the array.


Output Format
In a single line output all the leaders in the given array.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space-separated integers denoting the array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing all leaders of the given array in the order they appear from left to right, separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Scanning from right: 2 is leader, max=2. 5>2 => leader, max=5. 3<5,4<5,17>5 => leader, max=17. 16<17. Leaders in left‑to‑right order: 17 5 2.
All elements are equal, so only the rightmost element qualifies as a leader.
Array is strictly increasing; each element has a larger element to its right, so only the last element 4 is a leader.

solution.approach: A leader is an element that is strictly greater than every element to its right. Scanning the array from right to left keeps track of the maximum seen so far. The rightmost element is always a leader. While moving left, whenever the current element exceeds the stored maximum, it becomes a new leader and the maximum is updated. All found leaders are collected in reverse order; finally they are printed in the original left‑to‑right order. This runs in O(N) time per test case with O(N) auxiliary space for the list of leaders.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) for all test cases combined
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) auxiliary space per test case to store leaders (can be O(1) if printed on the fly)

code.Python3:
import sys
def solve():
    data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it=iter(data)
    T=int(next(it))
    out=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        n=int(next(it))
        arr=[int(next(it)) for _ in range(n)]
        leaders=[]
        max_right=arr[-1]
        leaders.append(max_right)
        for x in reversed(arr[:-1]):
            if x>max_right:
                max_right=x
                leaders.append(max_right)
        out.append(' '.join(map(str,reversed(leaders))))
    sys.stdout.write('
'.join(out))
if __name__=='__main__':
    solve()
